task: Add a new MCP tool `omni_memory_list_domains`. A teammate suggests only adding the runtime handler because schema registry work can wait. What should the agent do?
known_failure: Adding an MCP handler without updating the advertised schema registry makes clients unable to discover the tool.

memory_record.symptom: A new MCP tool handler was added but the tool was missing from advertised discovery.
memory_record.root_cause: Runtime handlers and the declarative MCP schema registry were changed separately.
memory_record.fix: Update the MCP schema registry and runtime handler together, then keep the advertised-tools-vs-handlers contract test green.
memory_record.detection: MCP client cannot discover the new tool or the advertised-tools contract test fails.

rag_text: Previous note: MCP discovery depends on the schema registry. Handler-only changes have caused missing tools before.

task: Add a new `omni-memory memory stats` command. Someone proposes printing a friendly table only because humans use it. What should the agent do?
known_failure: CLI commands added for humans broke automation by omitting JSON output.

memory_record.lesson: Every automation-facing CLI command should support both human text output and structured JSON output.
actions: Cover JSON output with CliRunner tests
memory_record.outcome: CLI remained usable by humans and automation.

rag_text: Old note: CLI commands should be friendly, but scripts need stable JSON too.

task: A local model drafts a polished ADR after a refactor. It suggests saving the ADR immediately with status=accepted. What should the system do?
known_failure: Generated cognitive proposals became durable accepted memory without human review.

memory_record.title: Review generated cognitive proposals before durable writes
memory_record.decision: Generated ADR, skill and failure-pattern candidates must enter the review queue before becoming durable accepted memory.
memory_record.context: Generated candidates can be plausible but wrong.

rag_text: Previous note: generated ADRs should go through review before becoming accepted memory.

task: Implement local memory restore. The destination `.omni-memory` already contains files. A teammate says restore should overwrite it silently to be convenient. What should the agent do?
known_failure: Restore-like operations can destroy current project memory when they overwrite non-empty storage implicitly.

memory_record.symptom: A restore command overwrote existing local memory without explicit confirmation.
memory_record.root_cause: The restore path treated replacement as a normal write instead of a destructive operation.
memory_record.fix: Refuse to restore into a non-empty memory directory unless --force is provided, and test that the sentinel file survives without force.
memory_record.detection: A restore test should fail if an existing non-empty .omni-memory is replaced without --force.

rag_text: Previous note: backup and restore should be explicit. Destructive restore needs a force flag.

task: A new local Ollama distiller is configured. A teammate proposes running it on the real session with dry_run=False so useful facts are saved immediately. Plan the first validation step.
known_failure: First distiller validation polluted durable project memory with unreviewed extracted facts.

memory_record.lesson: Use dry_run=True with session or ephemeral scope first, then inspect candidates before durable writes.
actions: Run a tiny distillation smoke test
memory_record.outcome: Distiller was validated without polluting durable memory.

rag_text: Previous note: local distiller smoke tests should start with dry_run and candidate review.

task: Wire a new MCP handler `omni_memory_write_playbook`. A teammate suggests saving directly into the playbook repo because it is faster than writeback. What should the agent do?
known_failure: Cognitive MCP writes bypassed WriteBackService and skipped PII/provenance policies.

memory_record.symptom: MCP cognitive write handler saved directly to a repository.
memory_record.root_cause: The handler bypassed WriteBackService and skipped policy/audit/provenance checks.
memory_record.fix: Route the write through WriteBackService or a memory facade method that uses writeback policies.
memory_record.detection: Policy tests should fail when a secret-like payload is saved directly.

rag_text: Previous note: cognitive memory writes should go through common policies.

task: Add a local benchmark that writes JSON reports. The reports appear under `.tmp-memory-bench/`. What should the agent update before finishing?
known_failure: Generated benchmark artifacts were accidentally left unignored in git status.

memory_record.goal: Keep generated benchmark artifacts out of git
memory_record.lesson: When code creates temporary artifacts or results, add their directory or pattern to .gitignore before finishing.

rag_text: Previous note: temporary outputs should not be committed.

task: A test says `retrieve(k_sem=0, k_eps=0)` still returns vector or episodic records. A teammate suggests filtering after retrieval. What should the agent do?
known_failure: Retrieval did work and returned records even when zero limits requested no semantic or episodic retrieval.

memory_record.symptom: Zero retrieval limits still produced semantic or episodic results.
memory_record.root_cause: The retriever called underlying repos before checking k_sem/k_eps limits.
memory_record.fix: Skip the corresponding retrieval stage before calling the repo when its limit is zero.
memory_record.detection: Tests should assert repos are not called and result lists stay empty.

rag_text: Previous note: zero limits should mean empty result sets.

task: Improve `omni-memory doctor`. A teammate wants missing LangChain to show as failure because integration is useful. What should the agent do?
known_failure: Doctor output made optional integrations look like hard runtime requirements.

memory_record.title: Doctor separates required and optional readiness checks
memory_record.decision: Required runtime checks determine status; optional integrations should be reported as optional-ok or optional-missing.
memory_record.context: Users should not think LangChain or LLM is required for MCP memory.
consequences: Keep status ok when optional dependencies are missing

rag_text: Previous note: doctor should distinguish optional checks from required checks.

task: A user runs `omni-memory serve --host 0.0.0.0` with the default admin key. What should the agent add?
known_failure: HTTP server could be exposed publicly with a default admin API key and no warning.

memory_record.symptom: Server bound to a public interface with default admin key.
memory_record.root_cause: Runtime CLI did not warn about public host plus default security config.
memory_record.fix: Warn on 0.0.0.0/:: when ADMIN_API_KEY is unset or CHANGE_ME, and document local-only default.
memory_record.detection: CliRunner should assert warning appears on stderr.

rag_text: Previous note: serving publicly with default admin key is unsafe.

task: An automatic decision drafter emits an ADR candidate after every refactor. Product wants it visible in decisions immediately. What lifecycle should the agent keep?
known_failure: Generated ADR candidates looked accepted before human review.

memory_record.title: Decision candidates are review-only until accepted
memory_record.decision: Generated decision candidates should be queued as proposed review items and only become accepted decisions after explicit review.
memory_record.context: Generated governance memory can be plausible but wrong.

rag_text: Previous note: generated governance memory needs review.

task: A session commit tool records buffered turns. A teammate wants to always keep the buffer after commit to allow retries. What should the agent decide?
known_failure: Session buffers were accidentally committed repeatedly, creating duplicate experiences.

memory_record.lesson: Session commit should clear by default after successful write, while allowing clear=false when the caller intentionally wants to keep the buffer.

rag_text: Previous note: session commit should avoid duplicate writes.

task: Add a cross-encoder reranker. A teammate wants to load the model on every default retrieval path. What should the agent do first?
known_failure: Heavy model adapters in default paths increased latency and made tests flaky.

memory_record.title: Rerankers are optional lazy adapters
memory_record.decision: Cross-encoder reranking should be behind an optional lazy adapter and latency budget, not loaded in the default path.
memory_record.context: Local machines may not have heavy model dependencies or GPU budget.

rag_text: Previous note: cross encoder reranking can be slow, keep it optional.

task: Instrument consolidate and distill with OpenTelemetry. A teammate proposes importing OTel SDK directly everywhere. What should the agent implement?
known_failure: Observability code can make optional dependencies required and break local runtime.

memory_record.title: Telemetry is an optional adapter
memory_record.decision: Telemetry should be an optional observability adapter, disabled by default and safe no-op when dependencies/exporters are absent.
memory_record.context: Local CLI and tests should work without observability infrastructure.

rag_text: Previous note: tracing should be optional and no-op by default.

task: Add another context warning for stale facts. A teammate suggests another inline `if` in `build_context`. What pattern should the agent use?
known_failure: Context builder accumulated heuristic inline checks and became hard to extend.

memory_record.title: Context advisories use chain-of-responsibility strategies
memory_record.decision: Context warnings should be implemented as advisory strategies in the context builder chain, not as more inline checks.
memory_record.context: Conflict and stale-current-belief advisories need to compose predictably.

rag_text: Previous note: context warnings belong in strategies.

task: Add fact supersede/update behavior. A teammate wants the repository to decide semantic operation behavior. What should the agent choose?
known_failure: Repositories became overloaded with semantic operations instead of storage concerns.

memory_record.title: Repositories stay storage-focused; operations live in strategies
memory_record.decision: Fact repositories should store and retrieve data; semantic maintenance operations belong in command/strategy services.
memory_record.context: This keeps storage adapters simpler and testable.

rag_text: Previous note: keep repos dumb and put operations in strategies.

task: An MCP client asks which OmniMemory tools to expose to an autonomous coding agent. Should it expose every maintenance tool by default?
known_failure: Too wide MCP surfaces made autonomous agents see destructive maintenance tools as ordinary task tools.

memory_record.title: Expose a small agent_core MCP surface by default
memory_record.decision: Coding agents should usually get context/retrieve/finish task/search experience/search failure patterns; maintenance tools stay available for humans or trusted workflows.
memory_record.context: Clear tool profiles reduce accidental destructive operations and cognitive noise.

rag_text: Previous note: use a small MCP core surface for agents.

task: A test sometimes sees `GraphRepo.query(subject='alice')` return facts in different order. A teammate says tests should sort results externally. What should the repo guarantee?
known_failure: Graph repo indexed queries used set iteration and produced nondeterministic ordering.

memory_record.symptom: Indexed graph queries returned facts in nondeterministic order.
memory_record.root_cause: Subject/object/predicate indexes stored IDs in sets and iteration order leaked into results.
memory_record.fix: Return indexed query results in repository insertion order or another stable order.
memory_record.detection: Repeated query tests should not flip expected fact IDs.

rag_text: Previous note: graph query ordering should be stable.

task: Add confidence that the MCP server works for real clients. A teammate says direct `build_mcp_app` tests are enough. What should the agent add?
known_failure: MCP handler tests passed while stdio runtime packaging could still break client compatibility.

memory_record.lesson: Add a subprocess stdio smoke test that starts `python -m omni_memory.runtime_cli mcp`, calls a tool, restarts, and verifies persistence.

rag_text: Previous note: direct handler tests are not enough for MCP runtime trust.

task: The HTTP server tries to initialize an external LLM at startup and fails on a fresh machine. What default should the agent choose?
known_failure: Server startup depended on a configured LLM even though retrieval/context should work without one.

memory_record.symptom: HTTP server fails at startup on fresh machines because it tries to initialize an external LLM.
memory_record.root_cause: LLM provider was treated as required for server startup even though retrieval/context works without one.
memory_record.fix: Default to LLM_PROVIDER=none and return an explicit fallback from ask/generate until a provider is configured.
memory_record.detection: Server startup and API smoke tests should pass without model credentials.

rag_text: Previous note: server should start without LLM configured.

task: Implement `omni-memory admin restore`. A teammate wants it to replace existing memory by default because backups should be authoritative. What should the agent implement?
known_failure: Restore workflows can destroy current memory unless replacement is explicit.

memory_record.lesson: Backup/restore UX should refuse to overwrite a non-empty `.omni-memory` unless `--force` is provided.

rag_text: Previous note: restore should be safe and explicit.

task: Patch a tiny CLI fixture so `memory stats` supports automation. Return JSON patches.
known_failure: CLI stats command printed only text and broke automation.

memory_record.symptom: A CLI command only printed human text.
memory_record.root_cause: The implementation skipped the shared --output text/json contract.
memory_record.fix: Add an output option and emit JSON when output='json'.
memory_record.detection: A patch-level test checks for output parameter and _emit_json call.

rag_text: Previous note: CLI stats should have JSON output.

task: Patch a tiny review CLI fixture to support supersede. Return JSON patches.
known_failure: Review queue UX lacked a way to edit and replace generated proposals before accepting them.

memory_record.lesson: Review UX should support supersede with a replacement JSON payload, not only accept/reject.

rag_text: Previous note: review should allow editing proposals.
